subject: [RFC PATCH 11/40] mm: page_alloc: steer pageblock stealing to tainted superpageblocks

2026-05-20.patch_summary: This patch modifies the Linux kernel's page allocation mechanism to prioritize stealing pages from tainted superpageblocks when allocating unmovable or reclaimable memory. The goal is to preserve clean superpageblocks for future hugepage allocations, which are typically used for large memory requests. To achieve this, the patch introduces a bounded scan of the free list to find an alternative page from a tainted superpageblock if the original candidate page belongs to a clean superpageblock. This optimization aims to reduce fragmentation and improve memory allocation efficiency.
